channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Micron CEO warns 'AI is in very early innings' and it will 'need more memory' — another ominous sign the RAM crisis isn't going anywhere
item.description: Micron has warned on the gravity of the RAM crisis, meaning that every major memory chip maker has now done so.

item.title: OpenAI’s cozy partner Cerebras is on track for a blockbuster IPO
item.description: AI chip maker Cerebras is heading for a blockbuster IPO that could value it at $26.6 billion or more. It's relationship with OpenAI is deep and rich.

item.title: Elon Musk settles with the SEC for $1.5 million after years-long dispute over his Twitter investment
The White House is considering tighter regulation of new AI models
item.description: A working group could vet new models before they're publicly released.

item.title: What to Know About Sony’s $7.85 Million PlayStation Settlement
item.description: Are you eligible for a payout? Probably, but it might take a while and will likely be pretty small.

item.title: 'Help cattle producers hold onto their land and livelihoods': This first-of-its-kind solar ranch in Tennessee uses ingenious sensors to shelter cattle and cut carbon emissions — and it could help to offset $1 billion of US agriculture declines
item.description: A Tennessee solar farm integrates cattle grazing using adjustable panels, offering potential income diversification as agricultural revenues decline across the United States.

item.title: ‘More powerful and versatile than any other personal fan I’ve tested’ — I reviewed Shark’s 3-in-1 portable fan and have never experienced so many different kinds of cooling
item.description: The Shark ChillPill is a portable cooling system that's essential for summer, travel, migraines, menopause and anyone else who gets too hot.

item.title: Microsoft continues the good work on Windows 11, with tweaks to 'quiet' ads — and that big taskbar change is 'coming soon'
item.description: I've had my doubts about Microsoft's campaign to fix Windows 11 this year, but they're slowly melting away.

item.title: Skylight’s 15-inch smart calendar is down to its lowest price to date
item.description: When you’re juggling more than just your own calendar, staying organized can be overwhelming. Fortunately, the Skylight Calendar 2 can help simplify things by syncing multiple calendars in a single spot. And right now, it’s on sale for a new low of $249.99 ($50 off) at both Amazon and Walmart, which is actually $10 less […]

item.title: Inside AMEX’s agentic commerce stack: How intent contracts and single-use tokens enforce AI transactions
item.description: American Express (Amex) is building a system that lets AI agents shop and pay on behalf of users — but right now it’s only within its own payment network, and still involves a black box that could hinder trust and auditability. Amex already participates in agentic commerce protocol projects, especially Google’s Agent Pay Protocol (AP2), which focuses on interoperability. Amex’s Agentic Commerce Experiences (ACE) developer kit, on the other hand, touches on something most protocols currently lack: Full transaction control in the payment layer. But it still isn't completely transparent in how it...

item.title: iOS 26.5 will add end-to-end encryption for RCS messages between Apple and Android
item.description: Keep those private conversations truly private.
